ranked_movies.title: Parasite
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as the perfect gateway into international cinema due to its masterful blend of genres, universal themes, and critical acclaim.

ranked_movies.title: Amelie
ranked_movies.reason: Consistently mentioned across multiple threads for its beautiful visuals, gentle and whimsical tone, and as a top recommendation for international films.

ranked_movies.title: Take Shelter
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended for Michael Shannon's intense and powerful performance, with multiple users citing it as a film that 'sticks with you'.

ranked_movies.title: Cinema Paradiso
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended in both the 'emotionally gentle' and 'international cinema' threads for its beautiful, nostalgic, and heartwarming story.

ranked_movies.title: Groundhog Day
ranked_movies.reason: Top recommendation for a film that encourages personal growth and becoming a better person, praised for its transformative and positive message.

ranked_movies.title: The Handmaiden
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently suggested as a thrilling and engaging entry point into Korean and international cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Sicario
ranked_movies.reason: Highlighted for its incredibly intense and speechless ending, with deep analysis of its narrative structure and Benicio Del Toro's performance.

ranked_movies.title: Moonrise Kingdom
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its emotionally gentle, whimsical, and visually beautiful style, fitting the request for a 'warm hug' of a movie.

ranked_movies.title: Revolutionary Road
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in two separate threads: for Michael Shannon's supporting role and as a portrayal of a powerless, miserable woman in a bleak life.

ranked_movies.title: In the Mood for Love
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a prime example of international cinema, specifically for its romantic and visually stunning Chinese storytelling.

ranked_movies.title: Perfect Days
ranked_movies.reason: Top comment recommendation for an emotionally gentle movie with calm pacing and beautiful visuals, described as a perfect fit.

ranked_movies.title: Pirates of the Caribbean: Dead Man's Chest
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily discussed for its groundbreaking and timeless CGI achievement in creating Davy Jones, with a highly upvoted post dedicated to it.

ranked_movies.title: Hardcore
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a deep-dive analysis on r/TrueFilm, praised for George C. Scott's performance and its fascinating, overlooked premise.

ranked_movies.title: The Blind Side
ranked_movies.reason: The most cited example of a film that was loved but is now widely hated and criticized, mentioned repeatedly in the discussion.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Toni Collette's performance was highlighted multiple times as one of the best in cinema history in the 'best actress' discussion.

ranked_movies.title: The Sixth Sense
ranked_movies.reason: Toni Collette's performance was also praised here, contributing to the discussion on the best acting performances by an actress.

ranked_movies.title: City of God
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a thrilling and accessible Brazilian crime film for those new to international cinema.

ranked_movies.title: Dancer in the Dark
ranked_movies.reason: Suggested as a film about a powerless woman in a miserable life, noted for its intense and difficult-to-watch nature.

ranked_movies.title: The Secret in Their Eyes
ranked_movies.reason: Specifically recommended as an excellent Argentinian mystery thriller for international cinema newcomers.

ranked_movies.title: The Breakfast Club
ranked_movies.reason: The subject of a deep-dive analysis on r/TrueFilm for its exploration of childhood neglect and lasting cultural impact.
